---
title: "Sebastian Kruber"
subtitle: "Postdoc"
image: sebastian_kruber.jpg
started: "April 2025"
toc: false
about:
  id: person-profile
  template: jolla
---

```{=html}
<nav aria-label="breadcrumb">
  <ol class="breadcrumb">
    <li class="breadcrumb-item"><a href="../../index.html">Home</a></li>
    <li class="breadcrumb-item"><a href="../../people.html">People</a></li>
    <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Research Staff</li>
    <li class="breadcrumb-item active" aria-current="page">{{< meta title >}}</li>
  </ol>
</nav>
```

:::{#person-profile}
:::

## Research Interests

- 4D-STEM imaging and analysis
- Computational image reconstruction
- Machine learning for electron microscopy
- Automated acquisition and reconstruction pipelines

## Brief Bio

Sebastian joined the ECLIPSE Lab as a postdoctoral researcher in April 2025. His work focuses on developing and applying 4D-STEM methods for high-resolution, dose-efficient imaging, with a particular interest in combining physics-informed models and machine learning to push the limits of what electron microscopes can reveal.

## Background

Sebastian holds a PhD in Physics from the Max Planck Institute for the Structure and Dynamics of Matter in Hamburg. His research experience spans laser physics, electron microscopy, and scientific software development.

**Contact:** sebastian.kruber@fau.de
